questions.text: Secondary activities are also known as:
questions.explanation: Secondary activities or manufacturing change raw materials into products of more value to people.

questions.text: Industry refers to an economic activity that is concerned with:
questions.explanation: Industries cover production (iron and steel), extraction (coal mining), and services (tourism).

questions.text: Industries classified on the basis of raw materials include:
questions.explanation: Raw materials can be agro-based, mineral-based, marine-based or forest-based.

questions.text: Dairy products and leather industries are examples of:
questions.explanation: Agro-based industries use plant and animal based products as their raw materials.

questions.text: Which industries use products from the sea and oceans as raw materials?
questions.explanation: Marine-based industries process sea-food or manufacture fish oil.

questions.text: Cottage and household industries are a type of:
questions.explanation: Cottage industries are small-scale industries where products are manufactured by hand, by the artisans.

questions.text: Industries owned and operated by individuals or a group of individuals are:
questions.explanation: Private sector industries are owned by individuals or groups.

questions.text: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) and Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL) are:
questions.explanation: Public sector industries are owned and operated by the government.

questions.text: Maruti Udyog Limited is an example of which sector?
questions.explanation: Joint sector industries are owned and operated by the state and individuals.

questions.text: Anand Milk Union Limited (AMUL) is an example of which sector?
questions.explanation: Co-operative sector industries are owned and operated by the producers or suppliers of raw materials.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a factor affecting the location of industries?
questions.explanation: Industrial location is decided by physical and economic factors like land, power, labour, and market.

questions.text: Major industrial regions of the world include:
questions.explanation: Major industrial regions are found in temperate areas, near sea ports and coal fields.

questions.text: Which of these is a major industrial region in India?
questions.explanation: India has several regions like Mumbai-Pune, Hugli, Bangalore-Tamil Nadu, etc.

questions.text: Which industry is often referred to as the 'Backbone of Modern Industry'?
questions.explanation: Almost everything we use is either made of iron or steel or has been made with tools and machinery of these metals.

questions.text: What is the correct input for the Iron and Steel Industry?
questions.explanation: Iron ore, coal and limestone along with labour and capital are the inputs for this industry.

questions.text: The process of melting ore at a very high temperature to extract metal is called:
questions.explanation: Smelting is the process in which metals are extracted from their ores by heating beyond the melting point.

questions.text: TISCO (Tata Iron and Steel Company) was started in 1907 at:
questions.explanation: TISCO was started at Sakchi, near the confluence of the rivers Subarnarekha and Kharkai in Jharkhand.

questions.text: Sakchi was later renamed as:
questions.explanation: Sakchi was renamed Jamshedpur after Jamshedji Tata.

questions.text: Pittsburgh is an important steel city of:
questions.explanation: Pittsburgh is a major steel-producing center in the United States of America.

questions.text: Where was the first successful mechanized textile mill established in India in 1854?
questions.explanation: The first mechanized textile mill was established in Mumbai due to its moist climate and port facilities.

questions.text: Ahmedabad is often referred to as the ________ of India.
questions.explanation: Due to its prominent textile industry, Ahmedabad is known as the 'Manchester of India'.

questions.text: Which city is known as the 'Manchester of Japan'?
questions.explanation: Osaka is an important textile center of Japan.

questions.text: Information Technology (IT) industry deals in the ________ of information.
questions.explanation: The IT industry is involved in storing, processing, and distributing information through technology.

questions.text: The Silicon Valley is located in:
questions.explanation: Silicon Valley is part of the Santa Clara Valley, located next to the Rocky Mountains in California, USA.

questions.text: Which city in India is the main hub of the IT industry?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is known as the Silicon Valley of India because it has the highest concentration of IT companies.

questions.text: The term 'sunrise industries' refers to:
questions.explanation: Sunrise industries are emerging industries like IT, Wellness, Hospitality and Knowledge.

questions.text: Which of the following is a forest-based industry?
questions.explanation: Paper, pharmaceuticals, and furniture industries use forest produce as raw materials.

questions.text: Large-scale industries are characterized by:
options: Production of large volumes of goods
questions.explanation: Large-scale industries (like automobiles) require more money and advanced technology compared to small-scale.

questions.text: Which sector is also known as the state-owned sector?
questions.explanation: Public sector industries are owned and operated by the government.

questions.text: The industrial system consists of:
questions.explanation: Like any system, industries involve inputs (raw materials), processes (manufacturing steps), and outputs (final product).

questions.text: Great Lakes of North America are important for:
questions.explanation: Iron ore from Minnesota is transported to Pittsburgh via the Great Lakes waterway.

questions.text: Purely 'Agro-based' industry is:
questions.explanation: Cotton is a plant-based raw material.

questions.text: Muslin of ______ was world famous for its quality.
questions.explanation: Dhaka's Muslin and Chintzes of Masulipatnam were highly valued before the industrial revolution.

questions.text: Which fiber was the first to be used in the textile industry?
questions.explanation: Cotton is a natural fiber used since ancient times; others are synthetic.

questions.text: Which river flows through Jamshedpur?
questions.explanation: Subarnarekha and Kharkai provide water supply to Jamshedpur steel plants.

questions.text: Steel is often called the alloy because it is mixed with small amounts of:
questions.explanation: Alloys give steel unusual hardness, toughness, or ability to resist rust.

questions.text: The first IT hub in India was:
questions.explanation: Bengaluru (Bangalore) was the pioneer in India's IT revolution.

questions.text: What does TISCO stand for?
questions.explanation: It is the oldest and one of the largest private sector steel companies in India.

questions.text: Why did the textile industry flourish in Ahmedabad?
questions.explanation: Ahmedabad is located in Gujarat, a major cotton-producing state with suitable climate and infrastructure.

questions.text: Which of these is a marine-based product?
questions.explanation: Fish oil is derived from marine organisms.
